summary: Italian opera composer (1834–1886), best known for La Gioconda (1876), which contains the famous ballet 'Dance of the Hours.' Taught Puccini and Mascagni at the Milan Conservatory. Also composed I Lituani (based on Adam Mickiewicz's Konrad Wallenrod). La Gioconda's libretto was written by Arrigo Boito.

works.name: La Gioconda
works.description: Ponchielli's most famous opera, premiered at La Scala in 1876. Set in 17th-century Venice. Libretto by Arrigo Boito (writing as Tobia Gorrio), based on Victor Hugo's play Angelo. The title character (a street ballad singer) helps her rival Laura escape the clutches of the Venetian inquisitor Alvise Badoero and his spy Barnaba. Ends with Gioconda stabbing herself. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Composed by Ponchielli; contains the 'Dance of the Hours' ballet and the aria 'Cielo e mar'
examples: name this composer of I Lituani and La Gioconda, the latter of which contains the 'Dance of the Hours'
'Cielo e Mar' is a highlight of this opera by Amilcare Ponchielli, which ends with the title character stabbing herself.

clues.clue: Title character (a street ballad singer) helps her romantic rival Laura escape the Venetian inquisitor Alvise Badoero and his spy Barnaba; Gioconda stabs herself at the end

examples: After La Cieca, the mother of this opera's protagonist, is saved by Laura, the protagonist decides to help Laura escape the Inquisition despite being her romantic rival.

examples: This composer...created an opera in which an old woman is accused of using witchcraft to influence a regatta.

examples: In this opera, two offstage voices shout about finding 'more corpses in the canal' during a final act that takes place in a dilapidated offshore palace.

works.name: "Cielo e mar" (Sky and Sea)
works.description: Tenor aria from Act II of La Gioconda, sung by Enzo on his ship as he awaits the arrival of Laura. The most famous aria from the opera. In 1982, Carlo Bini was roundly booed at the Met for singing it after replacing Placido Domingo onstage. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Sung by Enzo on his ship as he awaits the arrival of Laura
examples: While on a ship, Enzo sings this opera's most famous aria, 'Cielo e mar.'
Name this tenor aria which is sung in Act II by Enzo, who awaits the arrival of Laura aboard his ship.

clues.clue: In 1982, Carlo Bini was booed at the Met for singing this aria after replacing Placido Domingo onstage
examples: In 1982, Carlo Bini was roundly booed at the Met upon singing this aria after replacing Placido Domingo onstage.

works.name: "Dance of the Hours" (Danza delle ore)
works.description: Ballet interlude from Act III of La Gioconda, performed at a party at the Ca' d'Oro in Venice while Laura's husband Alvise believes she is dying of poison. Depicts the passage of time through four sections: dawn, day, evening, and night. Animated by Disney in Fantasia (with hippos and alligators). Provided the melody for Allan Sherman's comedy song 'Hello Muddah, Hello Fadduh.' Wikipedia

clues.clue: Ballet depicting the times of day (dawn, day, evening, night); performed at a party at the Ca' d'Oro in Venice
examples: That opera's third act is set in the Ca' d'Oro, where the characters watch a performance in which the times of day are depicted in dance.
A ballet depicting the passage of time is performed while Laura's vengeful husband believes she is succumbing to poison.

works.name: Other Works
works.description: I Lituani is an opera based on Adam Mickiewicz's poem Konrad Wallenrod (about a Lithuanian pagan captured by the Teutonic Knights); its St. Petersburg premiere was under the title Aldona. I Promessi Sposi (The Betrothed) is based on Alessandro Manzoni's novel.

comprehensive_summary:
Amilcare Ponchielli (1834–1886) was an Italian opera composer best known for La Gioconda (1876), which is his sole work to remain in the standard operatic repertoire. He is also noted as a teacher at the Milan Conservatory, where his students included Giacomo Puccini and Pietro Mascagni.

La Gioconda is set in 17th-century Venice. The libretto was written by Arrigo Boito (under the pseudonym Tobia Gorrio), who also wrote the libretti for Verdi's Otello and Falstaff and composed the opera Mefistofele. The plot centers on Gioconda, a street ballad singer in love with Enzo Grimaldo. Her mother La Cieca, a blind street singer, is saved by the noblewoman Laura, which causes Gioconda to help Laura escape from Laura's husband Alvise Badoero (a Venetian inquisitor) and the villainous spy Barnaba, despite Laura being Gioconda's romantic rival. Gioconda sings the desperate aria 'Suicidio' and ultimately stabs herself rather than yield to Barnaba. An early subplot involves an old woman accused of using witchcraft to influence a regatta. Act II features the tenor aria 'Cielo e mar' (Sky and Sea), sung by Enzo on his ship as he awaits Laura's arrival — in 1982, Carlo Bini was roundly booed at the Met when he sang this aria as a last-minute replacement for Plácido Domingo.

The opera's third act is set at a party at the Ca' d'Oro in Venice, where guests watch the 'Dance of the Hours' (Danza delle ore), a ballet interlude depicting the passage of time through four sections: dawn, day, evening, and night. A chorus sings 'Prodigio! Incanto!' to mark the transition between dawn and day; costume changes and lighting effects differentiate the sections. The Dance of the Hours was animated in Disney's Fantasia (1940), depicting dancing ostriches, hippos, elephants, and alligators, and its tune is the basis for comedian Allan Sherman's 1963 novelty song 'Hello Muddah, Hello Fadduh.' Christopher Wheeldon re-choreographed it for a Met production. In the original music, flutes play 'B4 B5 B4' to punctuate an ascending theme that begins G-sharp, B, B, C-sharp.

Among Ponchielli's other works, I Lituani is based on Adam Mickiewicz's poem Konrad Wallenrod about a Lithuanian pagan captured by the Teutonic Knights; its St. Petersburg premiere was given under the title Aldona. I Promessi Sposi is based on Alessandro Manzoni's eponymous novel.

cards.front: Opera: set in 17th-century Venice; libretto by Arrigo Boito; title character stabs herself at the end after helping her rival Laura escape the inquisitor Alvise Badoero and his spy Barnaba
Opera: Arrigo Boito wrote its libretto (he also wrote libretti for Verdi's Otello and Falstaff and composed Mefistofele)
Opera: title character's mother La Cieca is accused of using witchcraft to influence a regatta; her daughter sings the aria 'Suicidio' before stabbing herself
Opera aria: sung by Enzo on his ship in Act II as he awaits the arrival of Laura; Carlo Bini was booed at the Met in 1982 for singing it after replacing Plácido Domingo
Ballet: from La Gioconda; set at a party at the Ca' d'Oro while Laura's husband believes she is dying; depicts dawn, day, evening, and night
Ballet: animated in Disney's Fantasia with dancing hippos and alligators; provides the melody for Allan Sherman's 'Hello Muddah, Hello Fadduh'
Composer: I Lituani is based on Adam Mickiewicz's Konrad Wallenrod (St. Petersburg premiere under the title Aldona); I Promessi Sposi is based on Manzoni's novel

recursive_suggestions: La Gioconda (individual opera — Dance of the Hours, Cielo e mar, Suicidio)
Arrigo Boito (librettist — wrote La Gioconda, Otello, Falstaff libretti; composed Mefistofele)
